title: polygala vulgaris var. depressa
intro: Polygala vulgaris var. depressa, also known as Common Polygala or Carline Thistle, is a flowering plant in the milkwort family that is commonly found in Europe, Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can be attributed to its high content of polyphenolic compounds and saponins. Consuming the plant or using its herbal preparations has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in some traditional remedies as a powder or dried extract.
